verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is predominantly mitochondrial-inner-membrane-resident (F1 catalytic subunit, matrix-facing), but has a well-documented, functionally active dual localization on the plasma membrane in multiple cell types (hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, cancer cells). Surface pool is reached via active microtubule-mediated trafficking, localizes to caveolae/lipid rafts, and serves receptor functions (HDL uptake). Flow cytometry and confocal imaging confirm non-permeabilized cell surface detection. Ectopic pool activity is upregulated in tumor-like acidic/hypoxic microenvironments. This represents dual localization with its own transport mechanism, not passive recruitment.
